old_claim.note: Joint-Stock Companies provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No joint-stock dependency remains because individual Crown invention privileges are documented more than a century before the cited 1555 Muscovy Company.

source_shape.source_locator: The guide dates English and Welsh patent records from 1449 and describes the Crown and Chancery grant process through October 1852.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: English invention privileges originated as state grants within a royal administrative process.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later chartered trading corporation is a different monopoly form and cannot enable privileges already present in the record.

ontology_before: The early invention-grant system depended on a later joint-stock trading company because both involved monopoly rights.
ontology_after: Invention privileges and joint-stock trading charters remain distinct legal institutions.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_patent_systems no longer directly depends on joint_stock_companies.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a post-1555 corporate invention program whose operation required joint-stock organization.
short_reason: Joint-stock companies postdate the scoped grant tradition.
